_meta.description: Solution-level agentic AI umbrella — vendor self-attests to the listed cross-module AI capabilities operating consistently across modules in this submission.
_meta.source:
Caps 1.03 (LLM Layer) + 1.04 (Others) sourced verbatim from IMDA
"ERP-Wide AI Features" template provided by user 2026-06-03.
7 additional caps (cross_module_copilot through cross_module_recommendations)
drafted by Claude to surface common cross-module AI patterns in SG SME
ERP solutions (anomaly detection, predictive forecasting, agentic workflow
orchestration, IDP, AI-assisted reconciliation, recommendation engine).
All caps Preferred per IMDA's Importance column on the source rows.
_meta.ships_when: Caps populated 2026-06-03. Excel export wiring + DraftsContext editor support still pending — see TASKS.md.

umbrella_question: Does your solution incorporate any form of artificial intelligence that operates across some or all of your ERP modules in this submission?

caps.name: AI-Enabled Large-Language Model Layer
caps.description:
An intelligent interface enabling the access and translation of
complex, structured ERP data via prompting through natural language.

caps.name: Cross-Module AI Copilot / Assistant
caps.description:
An AI assistant that drafts and coordinates actions spanning multiple
ERP modules — for example, from a customer email, drafting a quote in
Sales, checking inventory in Supply Chain, and generating the AR
invoice in Financial, with human review at each handoff.

caps.name: Cross-Sub-Ledger Anomaly Detection
caps.description:
AI that flags unusual patterns by combining data across sub-ledgers
— for example, AP invoice anomalies cross-referenced with purchase-
order history, vendor master, and historical spend patterns to
surface potential duplicate payments or vendor master tampering.

caps.name: Cross-Module Predictive Forecasting
caps.description:
Forecasting that integrates inputs from AR aging, sales pipeline,
inventory levels, and AP commitments into unified projections such
as 13-week cash flow, demand forecasts, or headcount-cost runways
that draw on data from more than one module.

caps.name: Agentic Workflow Orchestration
caps.description:
An AI agent that coordinates multi-step workflows spanning modules
— for example, period close (drafts journal entries, runs sub-ledger
reconciliations, prepares variance commentary, assembles the board
pack) or new-vendor onboarding (validates registration, runs
compliance checks, creates the vendor master record, sends welcome
packet) with human approval gates between steps.

caps.name: Intelligent Document Processing (IDP)
caps.description:
AI that extracts structured data from documents (purchase orders,
vendor invoices, contracts, customer emails, expense receipts) and
routes the extracted fields to the appropriate modules with
confidence scoring and reviewer queues for low-confidence matches.

caps.name: AI-Assisted Cross-Ledger Reconciliation
caps.description:
AI that matches records across sub-ledgers — bank statements versus
cash sub-ledger, AR aging versus CRM opportunity stages, inventory
cycle counts versus sales velocity, payroll posting versus GL — and
surfaces matches plus exceptions for human review.

caps.name: Cross-Module Recommendation Engine
caps.description:
AI that recommends next actions based on context spanning modules —
for example, flagging customers likely to churn by combining payment
history (Financial), support tickets (FSM), and sales engagement
signals (CRM), or recommending re-order quantities by combining
sales velocity (Sales) with supplier lead times (Supply Chain).

caps.name: Other ERP-Wide AI Features
caps.description:
Please define any other cross-module AI capabilities your solution
provides that are not covered above. (Capture as free text in a
follow-up — schema does not yet support inline text input here.)
